滴答，所有「美梦剧团」都是为宾客服务的梦境机器，我们被筑梦师创造，由鸢尾花家系管理，和艺者们一同为梦境带来快乐。

Tick-tock, everyone in the Sweet Dreams Troupe are Dreamscape machines that serve our guests. We were created by our Dreamweavers, are managed by the Iris Family, and with our fellow artistes, bring joy to the Dreamscapes.
滴答，我们的团长…不能说，不能说，她说这是保密事项。
Tick-tock, our team leader... I can't say, can't say. She said it was classified.
总之她是一个很好的人，滴答，如果不是她，
In any case, she's a very nice person. Tick-tock, if not for her,
Where did these building blocks come from?
滴答，这个是剧团里的采购员工搞来的，说是从一个逐梦客小贩那里买的。
Tick-tock, our troupe procurer purchased these. It's said he bought it from some Dreamchaser merchant.
滴答…没想到他买来的全是问题产品，真是可恶，这家伙肯定是吃回扣了！
Tick-tock... Who knew he bought a bunch of faulty goods. Dang it, that fellow must've been getting kickbacks from this deal!
Tick-tock, thank you for your assistance!
滴答，太好了，这样我手下的所有迷钟应该都被修好了，谢谢您！
Tick-tock, that's wonderful! Now all the Dream Tickers in my team should be all up and running properly. Thank you!
滴答，但其他主管的员工应该也拿到了这种奇怪的积木…好心的宾客，如果您今后再遇到故障的迷钟，也请帮帮它们吧！
Tick-tock, but the other supervisors' personnel should have gotten these strange blocks too... Kind-hearted guest, if you run into any faulty Dream Tickers in the future, please help them out too!
滴答，好的，祝您在梦里玩的开心！
Tick-tock, okay! I wish you a wonderful time in the dream!
从迷钟们手中得到的积木堆放在此处，散发出一股不祥的气息。
The building blocks retrieved from the Dream Tickers have been piled here, and they emanate an ominous aura.
你似乎听到这些积木在跟你低语——「不要再扮演别人了」。
You seem to hear these building blocks whispering to you — "Don't pretend to be anyone else anymore."
你触碰积木的瞬间，一种古怪的念头涌上心头，你开始怀疑自己并不是{NICKNAME}，而是一个被洗脑的演员，生活在虚假的梦中。

The instant you touch the building blocks, a peculiar thought surges through your mind. You start to doubt that you're not {NICKNAME} at this point, but a brainwashed actor who is living in a false dream.
刚刚的念头逐渐消散…好险，你差点就要和那些迷钟一样开始发癫了。
The previous thoughts are fading away... That was close, you almost went mad like those Dream Tickers.
为了你的精神健康，还是不要接近这些积木为妙。
For your mental health, it's better not to get close to the building blocks.
你和你的狱友小心翼翼地在监狱走廊上奔跑，躲过一队又一队警卫……
You and your cellmate stealthily run along the prison corridors, narrowly avoiding guard after guard...
不知过了多久，通往汇合点的港口才终于出现在视野里——果不其然，航道已经被破坏了。
Some time has passed, and the harbor leading to the rendezvous point finally comes into view — To the surprise of no one, the lane has already been destroyed.
你明白，突破这最后的封锁，你和狱友就能与大部队汇合了。
You know that, once you break through this final blockade, you and your cellmate will be able to reunite with the main group.
自从哈努兄弟用苏乐达拉拢了数个区划的囚犯后，你们的队伍一天比一天壮大起来。
Ever since Brother Hanu used SoulGlad to rope in prisoners from several areas, your team has been getting stronger and stronger by the day.
典狱长石头老板对此大为震惊。他召回了所有狱卒，用坚固的铁门封锁了反抗区划的全部通道，似乎是打算直接饿死你们。

The prison warden, Boss Stone, is shocked by this. He recalls all the prison guards and seals all the paths in the rebel sectors with sturdy iron gates. He probably intends to just starve you all to death.
不过针对这件事，你的同伴们似乎想到了一个计划……
In response to this situation, your companions appear to have hatched a plan...
哈努兄弟拿到了监狱系统的控制秘钥，但只有一把——想要开启监狱电脑必须集齐全部四把。
Brother Hanu retrieves the secret passkey for the prison system, but there's only one key — You'll need all four keys to activate the prison computer.
交流分析过后，哈努兄弟决定前往核心区域，一次将剩下的三把秘钥全搞到手。
After a detailed discussion and analysis, Brother Hanu decides to head to the core area and retrieve the remaining three keys in one fell swoop.
在混乱的梦境中，你和狱友一阵东跑西颠，总算在一处梦境房间发现了你们要找的目标——哈努兄弟和跟班。
In the chaos of the Dreamscape, you and your cellmate run around endlessly, finally locating your target in one of the dream rooms — Brother Hanu and his men.
我们成功了。看，是哈努老大！哈努老大亲自来接应我们了！
We made it! Look, it's Boss Hanu! He's here to pick us up!
客人，之前您关于《哈努兄弟越狱记》的体验意见已经反馈给梦泡制作组了——几位筑梦师都非常激动呢。
Dear guest, the feedback on your playthrough experience for Hanu's Prison Break has been submitted to the dream bubble producers — several Dreamweavers are very excited!
他们托我将这枚梦泡转交给你，作为献给第一个忠实玩家的特别礼物。
They've asked me to pass this dream bubble to you as a gift for the very first loyal player!
据说这枚梦泡里面记录着《哈努兄弟越狱记》的删减片段，至于如何观看嘛...您多把玩几次就知道了。
Apparently, this dream bubble contains deleted scenes from Hanu's Prison Break. As for how to watch it... You'll find out after a few more rounds.
I'm here to investigate the truth!
您说话还挺…跳跃的。「真相」可是个很严肃的词，希望您能慎用。
Your tone is quite... animated. The "truth" is a very serious word. I hope you will wield it with more prudence.
报社的信息不是你发的吗？
You weren't the one who sent the agency's message?
我想揭露梦境酒店的真相！
I want to expose the truth of the hotel in the dreamscape!
啊，我想起来了——报社的那条信息确实是我群发出去的，只是以前几乎从来没人回应过我。
Ah, I remember now — I did send that agency message to the group, just that everyone mostly ignored me.
这里的客人对于所谓的真相根本毫不在意…我都快彻底放弃念想了。愿意来主动配合我一起调查的人，您还是头一个。
The guests here are ambivalent about uncovering this truth... I myself was about to abandon this mission. The first person who willingly answered my call to investigate was you.
我觉得那地方很不对劲。
I think there's something wrong with this place.
The Family is definitely hiding something!
对吧？我也有同感。虽然没有确凿的证据，但身为一名记者，有些时候直觉就是第一驱动力。
Right? I feel that way too. I may not have concrete evidence, but as a reporter, sometimes you have to rely on your hunches.
直觉并不总是能带我找到真相。但如果没有了它，那我怕是连调查的第一步都迈不出去。
I can't always count on hunches to help me find the truth. But if there wasn't one, I wouldn't even dare embark on the first step of this investigation.
我看得出来，您应该也是一位直觉敏锐的人——而且还富有正义感，这在匹诺康尼的梦境里可不多见。
I can see that you're someone who's also got acute senses, and you're ripe with righteousness too — a rare combination in Penacony's Dreamscapes.
我就是单纯喜欢冒险闯荡。
I'm just someone who likes poking around on adventures.
正义的化身降临在了梦境。
The avatar of righteousness has descended unto this Dreamscape.
很好，很好。我们现在有动力、有脑子、也有——
Good, very good. Now we've got motivation, brains, and...
——辛格勒的视线快速扫过了你握在手中的球棒——
Singler's eyes quickly sweep over that baseball bat you hold in your hand.
——也有，咳，实力。事不宜迟，只要您没意见，我们立刻开始行动吧。
...And, ahem, brawn. Let's not waste time — if you're up for it, we'll move out now.
在白日梦酒店被彻底封锁之前，我就听说过一些传言：在某些特定的时刻，那座酒店中会突然冒出<color=#dbc291ff>意义不明的奇怪画作</color>，甚至可能将迷途的宾客吸入诡异的梦境空间中。

Before the Reverie hotel was completely locked down, I overheard a few rumors: At certain hours, a <color=#dbc291ff>strange painting of ambiguous meaning</color> would suddenly appear in that hotel, and perhaps even mislead guests, sucking them into an otherworldly Dreamscape.
奇怪的是，凡是有过类似经历的宾客，事后在被人询问时却都回忆不起来自己遭遇的具体细节。我不相信他们的集体失忆是一种巧合——这一定跟家族有关。

What's stranger is that, when inquired, the guests who had experienced the incident couldn't recall what happened to them. I don't believe that their collective memory loss is a coincidence — it must have something to do with The Family.
{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我觉得<color=#dbc291ff>奇怪的画作</color>就是我们调查的突破口。
{F#Miss}{M#Mister}, I think that <color=#dbc291ff>strange painting</color> will be the key to our progress in this investigation.
实不相瞒，我通过调查知晓了一条潜进白日梦酒店的秘密通道…只是此前没有帮手，我不敢一个人贸然前往。现在有您在，我就可以彻底放心了。

Truth be told, I learned of a secret passage that leads into the Reverie on one of my investigations... But I was alone then, and didn't dare to venture forth by myself. Now that you're around, I can put my mind at ease.
等您准备好了就和我说一声吧，我们动身去白日梦酒店。
Let me know when you're all ready. We'll set off for the Reverie hotel then.
好，请跟我来吧。记得表现得低调些，别太招摇……
Okay, please follow me. And remember to keep a low profile, as we don't want any attention...
当然，等您做好准备再来找我吧。
Of course, come seek me out when you're all ready.
您准备好了吗？我们一起动身去白日梦酒店揭露真相吧。
Are you ready? Let's head out to the Reverie to unveil the truth.
好，请跟我来吧。记得表现得低调些，别太招摇……
Okay, please follow me. And remember to keep a low profile, as we don't want any attention...
当然，等您做好准备再来找我吧。
Of course, come seek me out when you're all ready.
…就是这个！漂浮在酒店走廊中的诡秘画作，甚至可能无缘无故地吞噬迷路的宾客……
...This is it! That creepy painting floating in the middle of the hotel hallway. And it may even devour lost, unwitting guests too...
它的存在，就是家族失去对梦境世界掌控力的铁证！
Its existence is iron-clad proof that The Family has lost control of their Dreamscape!
家族一定早就发现了在酒店内发生的异常现象，这些画作只是其中比较显眼的一例。
The Family must already know about the strange happenings in the hotel. These paintings are just one of the more explicit examples.
我猜测——当然也只是猜测——他们一开始想要对宾客们隐瞒这些异变的存在，但纸包不住火，情况开始逐渐失控的时候，家族决定干脆无限期关闭酒店……

My conjecture — and it's mere conjecture — is that they had already planned to keep these anomalies from the guests in the first place. But guests going missing are too conspicuous to hide. When the situation went gradually awry, The Family decided to shut down the hotel indefinitely...
您说的对，中间缺少的环节现在都是靠我的想象填上的。
You're right. I've had to use my imagination to fill in the blanks within this occurrence.
You've filled in a few too many blanks.
您说的对，我们需要证据。
You're right. We still need hard proof.
作为自诩「崇拜事实」的调查记者…我想现在只有一个办法了。
As the self-styled investigative reporter, the "Disciple of Truth"... I can only think of one way.
我们试着触碰一下它吧，{F#小姐}{M#先生}——不管这幅画背后有什么，我相信它一定能带我们接近被家族掩盖的真相。
Let's try to establish contact with it. No matter what happens behind that painting, I trust it will definitely bring us closer to the truth that The Family is trying to hide.
又是…一幅画？而且看上去跟刚才那幅一模一样。
Another... painting? And it looks just the same as the one just now.
真没想到，酒店里的异变现象居然还会套娃。真有趣，这里的环境越是诡异，我们就距离真相越近——我的直觉是这么告诉我的。
To think that the anomalies in this hotel also devour themselves, interesting. The environment here is even stranger, which means we're getting closer to the truth — that's what my hunch tells me.
再来一次，我大概就能习惯这种脾胃翻腾的感觉了吧？{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我们走吧。
One more time, and I think I'll probably get used to this topsy-turvy, nauseating feeling? Let's go, {F#miss}{M#mister}
唔…晕，好晕，非常晕…我这小身板，真的经不住这种折腾……
Ugh... Dizzy, so dizzy... This puny body of mine really can't take much more of this torment...
您看上去好像还很精神…真佩服您的体质。
You, on the other hand, look refreshed as all heck... I'm impressed by your constitution.
Phew... I, I can see that.
总而言之，我们先把刚才的信息拼凑一下，看看能得出什么吧？
Anyway, let's piece together the information we've obtained first. See what we've found so far?
…{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我想，我们这回真的找到了宝贵的线索。
...{F#Miss}{M#Mister}, I think, we've stumbled upon a really precious clue this time.
这位名叫汉密尔顿的家族服务生，他的字里行间都透着紧张。家族一定是给他指派了什么秘密任务——如果完不成的话就要将他除名，甚至…要灭他的口！

This Family attendant named Hamilton, his writing indicates much anxiety. The Family must have assigned some secret mission to him — his name would be struck off if he failed the mission, or worse... his head!
He didn't say anything like that...
…咳，抱歉，我的想象力又发作了。
...Ahem, sorry, my imagination got the better of me.
We still don't have enough evidence.
…咳，您说的对，抱歉，我的想象力又发作了。
...Ahem, you're right. Sorry, my imagination got the better of me.
我们还需要更多证据，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。如果您不介意的话…我们再进到那个异空间里探索一次吧？
We still need more evidence. If you don't mind... can we enter that unusual dimension and explore it once more?
哇、哇？！这、这是什么情况？这片空间…好像彻底乱套了！
Wh—Whoa!? Wh—What's all this? This space... It's like a total mess!
冷静下来，辛格勒…你是调查记者，你得保持冷静……
Chill out, Singler... You're an investigative journalist and you should stay cool...
咳，总之，{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我们先开始收集线索吧？你走前面，我会努力跟上……
Ahem, anyway, maybe we can start gathering clues, {F#miss}{M#mister}? You go ahead, and I'll try to keep up...
这是…笔记？是谁留下的？上面写了什么？是有用的信息吗？
Is this a... note? Who left it here? What's written on it? Anything useful?
看不清啊…可惜，再到其它地方找找看吧。
A shame that it's so scratchy... Well, let's check other places.
这片空间里的线索大概都收集完了吧？应该没漏什么东西了。
I think we've collected all the clues in this area, right? Looks like that's all of them.
{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我们该拼凑一下收集到的线索碎片了。当然，还是先离开这个地方吧…我感觉有点晕。
I think it's time to sort out the clues we've gathered so far, {F#miss}{M#mister}. Of course, we really should get out of this place... I'm feeling a bit woozy.
咦…这个空间好像变得不太一样了？
Wait... this place doesn't seem quite the same, right?
但我们的目标还是没变——集中精力寻找线索吧，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
But our target remains unchanged. Let's focus on finding clues, {F#miss}{M#mister}.
这片空间里的线索大概都收集完了吧？应该没漏什么东西了。
I think we've collected all the clues in this area, right? Looks like that's all of them.
我们出去拼凑现有的线索吧，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
Let's piece together the clues we've got, {F#miss}{M#mister}.
这些迷宫，好像变得越来越复杂了……
These mazes... They're getting more and more complicated...
但这难不倒您，对吧{F#小姐}{M#先生}？加油，继续寻找线索吧。
But that won't be a problem to you, {F#miss}{M#mister}, right? Let's keep searching for more clues.
这片空间里的线索大概都收集完了吧？应该没漏什么东西了。
I think we've collected all the clues in this area, right? Looks like that's all of them.
我们出去拼凑现有的线索吧，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
Let's piece together the clues we've got, {F#miss}{M#mister}.
呼…呼…光是看着这迷宫，我就已经…汗流浃背了啊。
Phew... I'm already sweating... just looking at this labyrinth.
全靠您了，{F#小姐}{M#先生}…我有预感，我们离真相非常接近了。
I'm counting on you, {F#miss}{M#mister}... I have a hunch that we're getting really close to the truth.
这片空间里的线索大概都收集完了吧？应该没漏什么东西了。
I think we've collected all the clues in this area, right? Looks like that's all of them.
我们出去拼凑现有的线索吧，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
Let's piece together the clues we've got, {F#miss}{M#mister}.
很好，调查到目前为止一切顺利——起码我们俩都还走得动路，也没有缺胳膊少腿。
Good, the investigation has been going well so far. At least we can still walk, and we haven't lost any limbs.
拼凑一下我们刚刚得到的线索吧，看看有没有什么进一步的发现……
Let's piece together the clues we just got, and see if we've made any progress with our findings...
嗯…那位服务生汉密尔顿进入了梦境酒店，而且周围的人都在用奇怪的眼光看他……
Hmm... That attendant, Hamilton, entered the hotel in the Dreamscape, and the people around him started looking at him funny...
我现在有充足的理由相信，这位服务生的经历一定跟白日梦酒店崩溃有关。无论家族希望指派他去执行什么秘密任务，他肯定没能好好完成……

I now have ample reason to believe that this attendant's experiences must be related to the Reverie's shutdown. It doesn't matter what secret assignment it was that The Family gave him, he definitely didn't manage to complete it...
…否则这里也不会变成这幅模样，对吧？
...Or this place wouldn't be in this state now, would it?
可惜，到目前为止，这些笔记里还没有记录什么重磅的内容。我们还得继续调查才行……
It's unfortunate that there have been no crucial clues recorded in these journals so far. We have to keep investigating...
走吧{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我们再试一次。
Let's go. We'll have to try again.
呼…真是累人。调查记者工作的艰辛，想必您也感受到了吧？
Phew... this is exhausting. I'm sure you know the hardships of an investigative reporter now, eh?
来吧，我们把这次获得的线索拼凑一下，看看汉密尔顿又有什么话要说。
C'mon, let's gather the clues we obtained this time, and see if Hamilton said anything else.
舒翁…这个名字我倒是没有听过。
Siobhan... This name isn't familiar to me.
但从笔记里能看出汉密尔顿对那个女人的崇拜——如果真的和他说的那样，这位舒翁在鸢尾花中的地位仅次于家主……
But in the journal, it's pretty obvious that Hamilton admires that woman. If it is as he said, this Siobhan wields authority second only to the head in the Iris Family...
…那么一定是她给汉密尔顿指派了任务。但任务的内容是什么呢？抹去宾客的记忆？压制梦境中的异变？寻找怪物滋生的源头？笔记里还是一概没提……

...Which means she must be the one who gave Hamilton the mission. But what exactly did the mission entail? Erasing a guest's memories? Suppressing the anomalies in the Dreamscape? Finding the source of the monster propagation? The notes reveal nothing on this...
老实说，{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我稍微有些不耐烦了。我不知道这位汉密尔顿在现实里是个什么样的人，但我觉得他还挺有当谜语人的潜质的。
Honestly, I'm getting a little impatient. I don't know who this Hamilton guy is in the real world, but I think he's got the potential to be one of the Riddlers.
但我们已经走到这一步了，半途而弃未免太过可惜…我们再试一次吧，{F#女士}{M#先生}？我相信您也一定对后续的发展十分好奇……
But since we're already here, it'd be a waste to give up now... Let's try one more time, {F#miss}{M#mister}? I think you're very curious about how all this will unfold as well...
我…{F#小姐}{M#先生}…我想我已经到极限了……
I... {F#miss}{M#mister}... I think I'm at my limit...
希望那位汉密尔顿先生这次能直击重点，别再写些没头没尾的东西了…赶紧、赶紧，趁着我还没晕过去，我们看看线索……
I hope that Mr. Hamilton will get to the point this time, and stop beating around the bush with his entries... Hurry. Before I faint, we must look at the clues...
I'm guessing he's a Masked Fool.
You can never be too wary of dishwashers.
汉密尔顿…这家伙，不管你现在在哪……
Hamilton... You annoying fool, no matter where you are right now...
皮皮西人被那张纸条气得七窍生烟——绝非修辞，而是字面意义上的「冒烟」——然后仰面朝天撅了过去。
The Pepeshi person is fuming with rage at the note — not a figure of speech, but literally steaming with smoke — and then she turns her face skywards and falls over.
这片梦中的伤心地…你猜她一时半会儿应该不会想再回来了。
This heart-wrenching land of dreams... You guess that she won't be coming back for a while.
…您好，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。上次让您看到了我失态的样子，实在是抱歉。
...Hello. I must apologize for displaying that misconduct with you the last time.
咳，我得承认，上次调查的*失败*的确给了我不小的打击……
Ahem, I do have to admit, the failure of the last investigation really hit me hard...
但是作为「真相的仆人」，我——一位敬业的调查记者——是绝对不会轻言放弃的。
As a servant of truth, I — a committed investigative reporter — will never abandon my creed so easily.
等我再次抓到家族的把柄，{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我们还可以再次联手，彻底粉碎家族的阴谋。
Wait till I dig up more dirt on The Family, {F#miss}{M#mister}. We'll join forces again and shred The Family's conspiracies once and for all.
辛格勒头顶的绒球竖了起来，脸上摆出一幅势在必得的表情。她的执着和自信获得了你的赞许。
The fluffy ball on Singler's head stands upright, her face a visage of determination. Her resolve and confidence have earned your commendation.
喔…没想到你就是编导口中的「场外嘉宾」。
Oh... I didn't expect you to be the "special guest" the director mentioned.
咳咳，你好，我是乌玛，家族的新闻主持人，最近匹诺康尼电视台陷入了一点…混乱的局面，编导让我来求助一位「场外嘉宾」，没想到原来是你。
*ahem* Hi, I'm Uma, a news anchor from The Family. Penacony TV has been caught in a bind lately, so the director sent me to ask for help from a "special guest." I didn't expect it to be you.
哎…我们现在都没法正常工作，所以…我们想拜托你尽快找出幕后黑手。
Alas... we're unable to work as usual, so... please help us figure out who's behind the issue as soon as possible.
Do I have to appear on the show?
不，我很抱歉，我们不是这个意思。
No, I'm sorry, but that's not the kind of help we need.
而且…眼下这个情况，电视台的传输信号被干扰了，直播是不可能的。你没看到现在路边的屏幕上放的都是些无限循环的录播画面吗？
Also, live broadcasting is currently unavailable due to transmission signal interruption. Haven't you noticed we're playing recorded footage on a loop on the roadside billboards?
注意到那些路边的屏幕了吗？家族的技术人员在努力抢修了，但还是有少量屏幕的信号源没能恢复正常……
Have you noticed the roadside billboards? The technicians from The Family have pulled out all the stops to fix the signal issue, but a handful of screens are still yet to be restored...
好吧，简单来说就是这么回事——电视台的信号被不明团体劫持了，它们自称是什么「惊梦电视台」，接连开了好几档节目。
To put it simply, Penacony TV's signal has been hijacked by an unknown organization. They called themselves the "Dreamjolt TV" and have aired several TV programs.
他们还扬言要揭露家族的丑行，戳穿匹诺康尼电视台捏造出来的假象——唔，实际上都是些恶心观众的内容。
They threatened to expose The Family's scandals and debunked the illusions fabricated by Penacony TV. In reality, the content they have shown so far has been nothing but disgusting crap.
编导说你有过人之处，所以台里想请你帮个忙，帮我们查出劫持电视台信号的幕后黑手，让匹诺康尼的电视网络回归正常！
The director said you were exceptionally talented. We need your help nabbing the culprit who has hijacked the station's signal and restoring the network to normal!
What do I need to do?
Don't worry. This thing is simple.
感觉好麻烦，我能拒绝吗？
Sounds like a lot of trouble. Can I say no?
帮个忙嘛！这事很简单，不会耽误你太久的。
Please do us a favor! It's simple and won't take up too much of your time.
我们已经定位到几处信号异常的终端了。现在，我们只需要一位本领高强的帮手前往那些地点，手动「清除」那些敌台的信号。
We've located several terminals with anomalous signals. Now, we need someone skilled to visit those spots and remove those signals manually.
在把这几处终端的信号纠正过来之后，我们台里就能定位到真正的幕后黑手——当然，最后这一步也要劳驾你出手解决了。
After fixing the signals from these terminals, we'll be able to find out the real mastermind behind this incident. Of course, we'll also entrust you with this final step.
听起来不过是一球棒的事。
Sounds like something that can be done in a jiffy.
嗯嗯，不愧是「场外嘉宾」，说话就是硬气。
对了，根据家族送来的情报，我们发现了一个有趣的现象——
By the way, we've noticed something interesting from the intel sent by The Family.
在和它们交手的时候，找准它们的弱点，就能夺取特定观众的收视率，这会让你在战斗中占据优势。
When you're fighting with them, you can focus on their weaknesses to gain more audience ratings. This will give you an edge in the battles.
虽然编导说你身手了得，可能用不着这些旁门左道…但有机会的话还是建议你试试，但说不定还能为你减轻点负担呢。
The director thought you might not need the tip since you were so well-trained... but I'd suggest that you give it a try. It can probably make things easier for you.
那么，电视台的事就拜托你了，「场外嘉宾」{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
Well, we're counting on you to rescue the station, {F#miss}{M#sir}.
屏幕上播放着某款苏乐达汽水的广告，尽管你分不清它究竟是哪种口味的，但直觉告诉你似乎有什么地方不对劲。
An advertisement for SoulGlad is playing on the billboard. You cannot tell which flavor it is, but your instinct tells you something is off.
你试图换台，播放乌玛和她的同事们录制好的节目。屏幕闪烁了片刻，却没等来你想要的频道与画面。
You try switching the channel to play the pre-recorded show done by Uma and her colleagues. The screen flickers for a while, but the channel you want still doesn't come on.
「这是哪个台？这是什么节目？」你感到疑惑。
"What channel is this? What's this show?" you wonder.
「██味苏乐达独家冠名播出！惊梦电视台王牌舞蹈节目：与猩共舞！」屏幕里传来节目的广告声，你不明白究竟什么人爱看这种节目。

"This show is sponsored exclusively by ██ SoulGlad! The Dreamjolt TV presents to you our primetime dancing show: Dancing with the Simians!" The introduction of the TV program blares from the screen. You have no idea what kind of audience this show targets.
今晚的主题是！香蕉皮之夜！
Our theme for tonight is "Night of the Banana Peel"!
Hey, you. Come on inside and dance!
屏幕上播放着…一个垃圾袋的特写？你揉了揉眼睛，思忖这会不会是户外垃圾桶的推销广告。
There is... a close-up of a bin liner on the screen. You rub your eyes, wondering if this might be an advertisement for outdoor trashcans.
镜头在娇小怯弱的垃圾与通体金黄的垃圾之间不断切换——好吧，你承认它曼妙的表皮对你的确很有吸引力，但你还是努力克制住自己升腾的欲望。

The camera keeps switching back and forth between a small bag of timid trash and another bag that is golden in color. You can't help being drawn to its dazzling appearance, but you make a conscious effort to control the emerging temptation.
你试图换台，播放乌玛和她的同事们录制好的节目，但屏幕闪烁了片刻后，却没等来你想要的频道与画面。
You try switching the channel to play the pre-recorded show done by Uma and her colleagues. The screen flickers for a while, but the channel you want still doesn't come on.
「诶？那袋金光闪闪的垃圾呢？怎么不播了？」你感到疑惑。
"Eh? Where's that golden bag of trash? Why don't they show it anymore?" you wonder.
「我就是吃这些东西长大～纯天然，有机～」拿腔拿调的声线将重音停留在「有机」这个词上，每个字结尾都夹杂着古怪的嘶嘶声，令你几欲作呕。
"I grew up eating these! I'm all natural and organic!" The affected voice emphasizes "organic" and ends every word with an odd hissing sound that makes you feel sick.
随后画面上出现了一个身形健硕的怪人，它自称「银袋山」，是「垃圾家系」的家主，也是惊梦电视台人气真人秀「与银袋山同行」的主角。

Then, a bizarre, muscular figure shows up on the screen. He introduces himself as Bagtrashian, the head of the Trashian Family. He's also the protagonist of "Keeping Up With the Bagtrashians," a popular reality show by Dreamjolt TV.
嘶～这身腱子肉我没有训练过～我们家天生就这样～怎么～你不相信吗？
Grrr... I don't have to go to the gym for this body! We're all born this way! You don't believe me?
这又不是我的错～你不相信啊～那你跟我一起上节目，这期就拍你了～
This isn't my fault! If you don't believe me, come on and join the show! You'll be the star of this episode!
屏幕上，一顶厨师帽吸引了你的注意力，它看来是如此地平凡又普通。但你知道，越是普通的东西越危险——
A chef's hat on the screen catches your eye. There seems to be nothing special about it, but you know that sometimes the more inconspicuous a thing is, the more dangerous it can be.
你试图换台，播放乌玛和她的同事们录制好的节目，但屏幕闪烁了片刻后，却没等来你想要的频道与画面。
You try switching the channel to play the pre-recorded show done by Uma and her colleagues. The screen flickers for a while, but the channel you want still doesn't come on.
「美食节目还能有什么幺蛾子？」你感到疑惑。
"What kind of surprises could there be in a gourmet show?" you doubt.
一个戴着厨师帽怪模怪样的恐龙出现在电视画面中，它咧开血盆大口用力地嘶吼着，每句话都像在狠狠地喷人。

A curious-looking dinosaur, donning a chef's hat, makes a most peculiar entrance. With a gaping maw, he shouts and yells. Each of his utterances spews forth with such ferocity and a cutting acidity that seems to scorch the very souls of his audience.
主厨喜欢用嘴巴里的火来喷人——然而这却是这档「地狱厨房」节目的名场面。
The head chef seems to enjoy the spectacle of spewing fire from his mouth at people, which is, incidentally, the main selling point of this "Inferno's Kitchen" show.
这根本不是人吃的东西！
I wouldn't feed this to my worst enemy!
因为…这是我这种纵横厨界几十年的老家伙，才有资格品尝的菜肴。
That is because... it's a kaleidoscope of gastronomic delectation, which only a proper geezer like me, a master chef with decades of culinary experience under the belt, is entitled to savor.
好！大！一！块！生！肉！就让我来烤烤你！
IT'S RAW!!!!!! WHAT! A! DREADFUL! CHUNK! OF! RAW! MEAT! Do I need to put you on a grill!?
主厨兼主持人将视线转向你，酝酿着喉间的火焰——
The chef-cum-host turns his eyes to you and readies the flames in his throat...
屏幕中正在播放一档名为「匹诺康尼偶像」的选秀节目，一位选手真诚地笑着等待评委给出结果。
A talent show titled "Penaconian Idol" is playing on the screen. A contestant is waiting for the evaluation result with a sincere smile.
节目保留了评判结果的悬念，镜头在职业观众的假笑与愤怒之间来回切换，令你感到不适，你决定——
To keep the audience in suspense, the camera alternates between phony smiles and irritated faces of the professional audience. Feeling perturbed, you decide to...
你试图换台，播放乌玛和她的同事们录制好的节目，但屏幕闪烁了片刻后，却没等来你想要的频道与画面。
You try switching the channel to play the pre-recorded show done by Uma and her colleagues. The screen flickers for a while, but the channel you want still doesn't come on.
会唱歌有什么用，你都没有嚎啕大哭，也没有讲述你的悲惨身世。没有节目效果我怎么让你过？
It's not enough to just sing well. Without tearing up or narrating the story of your miserable life, you add nothing to the show. How can I let you through?
刁钻的评委将视线转向你。
The crafty judge shifts their gaze toward you.
下一位选手——{NICKNAME}，是吧？说吧，你的梦想是什么？
Next up! {NICKNAME}, right? Tell us about your dream.
在赶走了入侵电视台的四位「惊梦电视台」成员后，家族排除到了幕后黑手所在的位置。
After getting rid of the four Dreamjolt TV employees who have invaded the station, The Family has located the mastermind.
这片组合屏幕可真够大的…你有点看不懂这究竟是什么图案。
This is a massive spliced screen... There is a pattern on it that you don't recognize.
就在你靠近屏幕时，眼前的画面却突然模糊了起来。你头晕目眩，眼前闪过许多节目的画面，耳畔同时响起的几个嘈杂的声音还在不断讨论着什么……

As you edge closer to the screen, the image on it suddenly blurs, leaving your head spinning. The scenes of multiple TV programs are flashing before you, and you can hear a clamorous discussion going on nearby...
你冷静下来，深深地吸了几口气，抬头看，却发现一道可怖的身影出现在屏幕上。
You calm yourself down by taking a few deep breaths. By the time you look up again, you find a fearful figure emerging on the screen.
这究竟是什么？你不理解，也不敢多看。
What exactly is that? You have no clue and dare not linger your eyes on it.
你短促地抬头望了一眼，那些聒噪的声画便再度出现了。你瞬间感到心烦无比，只觉得莫名地升腾起一肚子怒火——
You cast a fleeting glance at it, and soon the cacophonous scene returns, driving you up the wall...
之前不是都见过面了？别太拘谨，放松。这里是冷清了点，不过正好，适合无所顾忌地谈话。
Didn't we already meet before? You should loosen up a bit. The place is quiet, but that's okay — we can say whatever we want here.
后来你跟加拉赫聊得还愉快吧？别看他老是漫不经心的样子，心里头埋着的事可多了。要不然，也不会成为这家酒吧的常客。
Did your conversation with Gallagher last time go well? He might look like he doesn't care about anything, but he actually has a lot on his mind. Otherwise, he wouldn't be a regular here.
我的酒吧别的没有，快乐可还真不少。
My place offers nothing but joy.
噢，原来你是直奔主题的类型？单纯点是好事，但太直接有时会不太讨喜哦？
Oh, I see you like to be straightforward. It's good to keep things simple, but you might get on people's nerves for being too direct.
…开个玩笑，我就喜欢像你这样务实的个性。
...I'm kidding. I like how pragmatic you are.
把我喊出来就为了说这个啊？
Did you ask me out just to tell me that?
What do you want to talk about, then?
聊天不过是拉近关系的方式，只要达到了目的，具体聊什么并不重要，不是吗？
Chatting is just a way to get closer to each other. As long as the conversation fulfills its purpose, it doesn't matter what we actually talk about. Don't you think so?
好啦，我猜你差不多也放松点了。特意请你过来，确实是有工作需要帮忙。
Alright, you seem more relaxed now. I invited you here because I needed your help.
见识过加拉赫的本事，不知道你对调饮这门手艺有没有更感兴趣——实不相瞒，我有些临时的事务缠身，得离开酒吧一段时间，这里缺个临时顶班的调饮师。

Are you interested in the art of mixing drinks, especially after seeing how well Gallagher does it? Something unexpected has come up, and I need to be away for a while. I'm looking for someone to take my place temporarily.
我觉得你很有做这行的天分，想请你替我顶会儿班。有兴趣吗？
I think you've got the talent to ace the job. What do you say?
直觉。再说了，看中一个人需要理由吗？
I just go with my gut. Must I have a reason for that?
我的眼光向来不会出错，你肯定能成为相当优秀的调饮师。
I've always had a good eye for talent, and I believe you'll be an awesome drinksmith.
来，先试试吧。别紧张，我会手把手教你的。
Come on and try it. Don't be nervous, I'll guide you step by step.
稍微教教就会，挺不错嘛。技术方面算是合格了。
You're getting the hang of things already! Technique-wise, you're a pass.
It's only because you teach well.
事先说清楚，现在奉承我可没有奖励——当然了，我的心情确实会变好。
Just to be clear, flattery will get you nowhere. It does make my mood better though.
别着急，我要说的才刚刚开始呢。
Don't get ahead of yourself. We're just getting started.
技术是成为调饮师的必备条件，但想要调出真正让人满意的饮品，需要的远远不止这个。
Knowing the mixing technique is the prerequisite to becoming a drinksmith. However, it takes more than that to serve drinks that impress your guests.
优秀的调饮师所需要具备的、最重要的技能，是聆听——<color=#dbc291ff>调饮…就是与客人对话的过程。</color>
The most crucial skill that a good drinksmith needs is listening... <color=#dbc291ff>Drinksmithing... is all about having conversations with your guests.</color>
饮品是种神奇的东西。就凭这一小杯漂亮的液体，辅以舒适的氛围、再加上一点点聊天技巧…它就能轻易敲开对方的心扉，毫不费力。
Drinks are magical. A beautiful glass of liquid, a cozy ambiance, and a little bit of conversational skills are all you need to make someone open up.
听起来有些玄乎，但我相信你拥有这样的能力。我早就看出来了，你是个善于共情的人…不仅如此，你能看透客人们的内心，甚至*调整*他们的情绪。

This might sound a bit esoteric, but I believe you have the knack for it. I can tell you're someone with empathy... Not only that, but you can also read someone's mind and even *regulate* their emotions.
你怎么知道我有超能力的？
How did you find out about my power?
在这里工作得久了，自然会懂得观察。理解顾客可是每位调饮师的必修课。
I've learned to pick things up from my experience here. Being able to understand the guests is a must for every drinksmith.
喂喂，你这也太抬举我了。
呵。那就当是我言过其实吧，但你确实很擅长和人交流，不愿意承认吗？
Well, I might be exaggerating things, but I'm sure you know how good you are at connecting with people.
上次我都看到了，你和艾米绮聊得很开心。她的性格我最清楚，黏人得很，让她安静离开可不容易。所以，我相信你能胜任这份工作。

I saw you having a good time talking to Amiky last time. I know her well — she can be clingy, and it's not easy to convince her to leave without making a fuss. So, I believe you're the right fit for the job.
怎么样，不说话就当你答应了哦？
What do you say? I'll take your silence as a yes.
哈哈，满分的幽默感。不错不错，你果然很适合做调饮师！
Haha, you've got a great sense of humor. You're really cut out for this!
要是你真的一点兴趣没有，那又是什么驱使你来到这里的呢？多余的感情？
If you aren't into mixing drinks in the least bit, what made you come here? Your overflowing sentiments?
哈哈哈。既然都跟我学了调饮，就没有让你离开的选择了。安心留在这吧。
Hahaha. Since you've decided to learn from me, there's no backing out now. Just chill out and make yourself at home here.
…在正式开始工作前，有件事必须告诉你。这家酒吧——正式名字叫「惊梦酒吧」——确实有它的特别之处。
...Before we start, I need to give you a heads-up. The name of this place is "Dreamjolt Hostelry," and there's something unique about it.
这间酒吧的顾客并非匹诺康尼的宾客或逐梦客，而是<color=#dbc291ff>生活在梦境酒店里的怪物。</color>
At this lounge, we don't serve the guests or Dreamchaser in Penacony, but the <color=#dbc291ff>monsters staying in the hotel in the dreamscape.</color>
它们原本是为家族服务的美梦剧团成员，但…我想你应该知道了，这片梦境正在坍塌，它们受到负面情绪的熏染，脾性发生改变，成为了难以控制的怪物。

They used to be the members of the Sweet Dreams Troupe that served The Family... As you should know, the Dreamscape is collapsing. The negative emotions have altered the temperament of the troupe members, turning them into uncontrollable monsters.
我留在这里，就是为了帮助它们稳定情绪，恢复到原本的模样。这是个漫长的过程，如果问题得不到解决，它们会重新陷入狂乱。我不想看到这种事发生。

I stay here to help them stabilize their emotions and restore them to their original form, which can be a long process. If the issue isn't resolved, they'll revert to the frenzied state, which is the last thing I want to see.
我不在的时候，这些怪物就要拜托你了。
Please take care of the monsters when I'm not around.
需要做的事情都差不多，也算不上是欺骗吧？
Since your job scope is more or less the same, it doesn't count as fraud, does it?
我是怪物保护协会的成员。
I'm a member of the Monster Protection Society.
有这种协会么？不介意的话，让他们来我这团建呗，我正好也见见。
Is there such a society? If you don't mind, you can invite your fellow members here for team building. I'd love to meet them.
当然，那些怪物没什么攻击性，不会伤害你，只是…性格上有点麻烦，需要多点耐心。
Well, those monsters aren't aggressive, so they won't hurt you. It's just that... their personalities can be a little abrasive, so you need to be more patient.
它们的具体情况都记在这本<color=#dbc291ff>问题手册</color>里，你可以翻翻看，在接待过程中有什么需要备忘的，也可以随时写进去。
You can find details about their conditions in this <color=#dbc291ff>Troubleshooter</color>. Feel free to flip through it and jot down stuff in it.
<color=#dbc291ff>为怪物们提供需要的饮料，获得信任，进而解决它们的情绪困扰</color>——这就是「惊梦酒吧」的调饮师需要完成的工作。
<color=#dbc291ff>Making drinks for the monsters to gain their trust and help with their emotional troubles</color> is the job of a drinksmith at Dreamjolt Hostelry.
哈哈，也可以这么说？和怪物深入交流的机会可不是哪都有的，算是特色了吧。
Haha, you can put it that way. The chance to have in-depth conversations with monsters doesn't come by every day. Consider it a perk.
交给我的麻烦事真多啊。
Sounds like I'm taking up quite an onerous task.
哈哈，这不是能者多劳嘛。你不来当这里的调饮师可实在太浪费了。
Haha, with great talent comes great responsibility! It would be a waste if you didn't work as a drinksmith here.
其实要做的事还是那样，倾听怪物们的需求，为它们提供想要的饮料。可能的话，尽量陪它们多聊聊。它们很怕寂寞。
Actually, your job is pretty much the same — to listen to the needs of the monsters and provide suitable drinks for them. Try talking to them as much as possible. They really hate to be lonely.
还记得我说的话吗，饮品是种很神奇的东西，能打开对方的心扉。调饮能做到的事，远比你预想得要多。
Still remember what I said just now? Drinks are magical things that can make someone open up. What a drinksmith can achieve is so much more than you might expect.
好啦，后面就交给你了。「惊梦酒吧」…新来的调饮师？
Now, I'll leave the Dreamjolt Hostelry under your care...
（只接待了两个客人，却憋了一肚子的火！）
(I'm about to blow a fuse already just after serving two customers!)
...This is by far the most infuriating thing!
I feel like teaching them a lesson!
哈哈，刚开始是这样。别太往心里去。怪物们受噩梦的侵袭太深，性格已经变得奇怪了。
Haha, it's normal to feel that way in the beginning. Please don't take it to heart. They're just too affected by the nightmare that they can be a bit twisted.
在过去，它们的情绪还正常的时候，都是相当乖巧懂事的孩子，很讨人喜欢。
When things were all good in the past, they were a bunch of lovely and sensible kids.
能接纳这些怪物，和它们平等交流…我就很感激了。
I'm already grateful because you can accept these monsters and treat them as equals.
I only lend my ear to them.
你回应得也很不错。能接纳这些怪物，和它们平等交流…我就很感激了。
And you respond to their issues well. I'm already grateful because you can accept these monsters and treat them as equals.
嗯，是时候教你「进阶课程」了。
Hmm, it's time to give you the "advanced lesson."
调制饮品讲究创意，需要根据时机与客人们的喜好来制作独特的饮品。
A creative mind is key to mixing drinks. You need to come up with special blends based on the time and your customer's preferences.
等我把「自由调制」的秘诀传授给你，你就能制作出酒单上不存在的饮品了。这可是每位调饮师的必修课——总给客人喝卖相、成分全都一成不变的饮料，换谁都会腻的。

After I show you the ropes about "Free Mixing," you'll be able to craft drinks that aren't on the menu. This is something every drinksmith has to learn. Things could get boring if you keep dishing out the same recipes.
比如说，我现在想喝<color=#dbc291ff>大杯特别甜</color>，能够让人回忆起过往幸福时光的饮料——
For instance, I want a <color=#dbc291ff>large, extra sweet</color> drink that brings back happy memories of my past...
酒单上的品类可满足不了这种需求，你要怎么处理呢？
Nothing on the menu fits the requirements. What should you do now?
Hmm... Let me think about it.
Don't panic. Let me show you what to do.
相当出色！你对味道的把握很敏锐，同时能准确洞察对方的内心…说不定，有天我都得请你为我调制适合的饮品。
Excellent! Your grasp of flavors is on point, and you can accurately perceive what's on someone's mind... I might ask you to make a drink for me one day.
后面我要暂时离开，但怪物们还是会定期光顾，酒吧就得拜托你打理了。缺少了这里提供的饮料，我担心它们的情绪会变得不稳定。
I'll be gone for a while, but the monsters will still drop by from time to time. Please tend the lounge for me. Without the drinks here, I'm worried those monsters might get angsty.
好啦，工作了这么久，你肯定也累了。先去休息吧。
Now, you must be drained after a long shift. Take a break.
（整天接待这种奇奇怪怪的客人还真挺累的，调饮师的工作果然不好做啊。）
(Dealing with these weirdos day-in, day-out is indeed quite tiring. Being a drinksmith is certainly no easy feat.)
（果然舒翁不在店里…直接开始营业吧。）
(As expected, Siobhan is not around... Let's open for business.)
（咦，怎么好像听到有打架的声音……）
这是我最珍贵的收音机汪！就被你这么弄坏了。要赔，全赔给我哇！
I love this radio, woof! You broke it! I want you to pay for it!
噢。抱歉，我不是故意要这么做的，只是…不小心，手劲大了点，就捏碎了哈哈哈！你不会要掉小珍珠了吧？多来点，多来点！
Oh. I'm sorry, and I didn't mean to break it. I just... held it too tightly and crushed it by accident, hahaha! Are you going to cry? More, please!
汪！我好心把收音机借给荷官先生玩，没想到…它直接就朝地板上摔了哇！
Woof! I lent my radio to the dealer out of kindness, but he smashed it on the ground!
没有摔，没有摔…准确说，是捏。我就稍微用了点力，它就碎了。太脆弱了，这可不行。
There was no smashing of any kind... To be more precise, I only pinched it, and it shattered after I applied a little pressure. How fragile.
哇嘎嘎嘎嘎，你看，要是它比地板更硬，是不是不会有事了？对吧！
Woah, woah, woah. Did you hear him? It wouldn't have happened if the radio was harder than the floor, would it?
呜——你就是存心捉弄我，把你咬成碎片汪！
*whimper*... You're clearly messing with me. I'll tear you into pieces, woof!
我是这里的调饮师，都听我的！
I'm the drinksmith, and I call the shots here!
呜呜…新人，我好委屈，我是冤枉的，你可得帮我评评理呀。
*sob*... I'm being framed, newcomer. You've got to help me out.
汪！我什么都没做，全部都是荷官先生的错哇！
Woof! I didn't do anything! It was all his fault!
（它们都不承认自己做错了，还是分别去问问究竟发生了什么吧。）
(Neither of them admits to any wrongdoing. I should probably ask them separately.)
我、我很无辜的呀。谁叫那个收音机…突然发出那么大的噪音，震得我的小心脏生疼生疼的，脚一滑，手一抖，就摔地上了。我也是受害者呜呜。
I—I'm innocent. That radio just randomly started blaring out loud noise. I was startled and slipped. My grasp on the radio loosened, and then it dropped. *sob* I'm a victim too.
再说嘛，就是个收音机，干嘛这么在乎。想听点啥…嘿，不如，我来讲给它听。
Besides, it's just a radio. What's the big deal anyway? If he wants to listen to something... Heh, I'll give him something to listen to.
恐怖的搞笑的正经的不适宜播出的，我啥都能讲，哇嘎嘎，尤其擅长讲狗狗的笑话！
I can talk about all kinds of stuff: Scary, funny, serious, or even the kind of stuff you can't put on the airwaves. Whoa now, I'm also quite the expert when it comes to canine jokes!
你…是认真的吗？你希望…我，如此善良热情的乖孩子，去向那只傻狗低头认错？
Are you... serious? You want me... an innocent and passionate kid to bow my head and apologize to that idiot pooch?
我好委屈呜呜。我当然知道自己做了什么，也分得清对错，坦率跟你说…这次的事，我都是故意的哈哈哈哈哈哈哈！
